items.description: schedule_tasks accepts a parameter 'tasks' which is a list of unique task names (strings).
items.expected_behavior: Function accepts a list of strings as 'tasks' parameter without error.

items.description: schedule_tasks accepts a parameter 'dependencies' which is a list of pairs of strings representing dependency pairs (before_task, after_task).
items.expected_behavior: Function accepts a list of tuple pairs of strings as 'dependencies' parameter without error.

items.description: schedule_tasks returns a list of strings representing a valid ordering of all tasks.
items.expected_behavior: Return value is a list of strings containing all tasks in a valid order.

items.description: Every task in the input 'tasks' list appears exactly once in the returned execution order.
items.expected_behavior: Returned list contains all tasks exactly once, no duplicates or omissions.

items.description: For every dependency pair (A, B), task A appears before task B in the returned order.
items.expected_behavior: In returned list, each 'before_task' precedes its 'after_task' for all dependencies.

items.description: The output ordering is deterministic: when multiple tasks are available, the lexicographically smallest is selected first.
items.expected_behavior: Ordering is consistent and lexicographically smallest task is chosen first among available tasks.

items.description: If the 'tasks' list contains duplicate task names, schedule_tasks raises a ValueError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises ValueError on duplicate task names in 'tasks'.

items.description: If any dependency refers to a task not present in the 'tasks' list, schedule_tasks raises a ValueError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises ValueError if any dependency references unknown tasks.

items.description: If a circular dependency exists among tasks, schedule_tasks raises a ValueError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises ValueError on detection of circular dependencies.

items.description: If the 'tasks' parameter is not a list, schedule_tasks raises a TypeError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises TypeError if 'tasks' is not a list.

items.description: If the 'dependencies' parameter is not a list, schedule_tasks raises a TypeError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises TypeError if 'dependencies' is not a list.

items.description: If any task name in 'tasks' is not a string, schedule_tasks raises a TypeError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises TypeError if any element in 'tasks' is not a string.

items.description: If any dependency is not a pair of strings, schedule_tasks raises a TypeError.
items.expected_behavior: Function raises TypeError if any dependency is not a tuple of two strings.

items.description: If 'tasks' and 'dependencies' are both empty, schedule_tasks returns an empty list.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns [] when both inputs are empty.

items.description: If there is a single task with no dependencies, schedule_tasks returns a list containing only that task.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns [task] when one task is given with no dependencies.

items.description: If there are multiple tasks with no dependencies, schedule_tasks returns them in lexicographic order.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns all tasks sorted lexicographically when no dependencies exist.

items.description: For a long dependency chain, schedule_tasks returns tasks in dependency order.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns tasks ordered respecting the full chain of dependencies.

items.description: For multiple independent chains, schedule_tasks produces a deterministic ordering based on lexicographic availability.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns a deterministic order choosing lexicographically smallest available tasks across chains.

items.description: For a diamond-shaped dependency graph, schedule_tasks returns an ordering where downstream tasks appear after all their prerequisites.
items.expected_behavior: Function returns an order where tasks downstream in diamond shape appear after all their dependencies.

items.description: Task names are case-sensitive.
items.expected_behavior: Function treats task names with different cases as distinct tasks.

items.description: schedule_tasks returns only the ordering and does not execute tasks or perform any file or external side effects.
items.expected_behavior: Function produces no side effects such as file, network, or process operations.

items.description: schedule_tasks has expected time complexity O((N + E) log N), where N is number of tasks and E is number of dependencies.
items.expected_behavior: Function runs within expected time complexity for large inputs.

items.description: schedule_tasks has expected space complexity O(N + E), where N is number of tasks and E is number of dependencies.
items.expected_behavior: Function uses space proportional to number of tasks and dependencies.
